meta.title: Paw Tag | The companion safety and wellness ecosystem
meta.description: Protect your companion with Paw Tag. Access their location, medical history, and digital documents instantly. Join the community of responsible owners.

hero.subtitle: It's not just a collar. It's a digital passport connecting medical history, location, and your unconditional love in a single tap.

beneficios.subtitle: An ecosystem designed from respect and love, providing peace of mind in every moment of the day.
beneficios.card1_desc: Whoever finds your companion only needs to bring their phone close to see your contact and location.
beneficios.card2_desc: Vaccines, allergies, and treatments instantly accessible for any veterinary emergency.
beneficios.card3_desc: Forget paperwork. Carry registration, insurance, and travel certificates in your pocket.
beneficios.card4_desc: Connect with other responsible owners, discover pet-friendly parks, and receive neighborhood alerts.

como_funciona.subtitle: Advanced technology, designed to be intuitive.
como_funciona.step1_desc: Receive your smart tag with exclusive Paw Tag design.
como_funciona.step2_desc: Bring your phone close to the tag and create your companion's profile for free.
como_funciona.step3_desc: Place it on their collar. Their vital information is now protected.

veterinarias.title: Do you own a pet-related business?
veterinarias.subtitle: Join the official Paw Tag network. Clinics, hospitals, pet shops, grooming salons, daycares, and walkers: offer your clients the most advanced identification and give an added value to your services.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: We answer your questions about the Paw Tag ecosystem.
faq.a1: Paw Tag goes far beyond a simple tracker; it's a complete ecosystem for responsible owners. In addition to tracking technology via a "Proximity Network" (similar to other high-end trackers), it includes a complete digital medical passport. From a single app, you can manage your companion's clinical history, save important documents, and connect with an active community, all designed from love and respect for their well-being.
faq.q2: How do I link my account?
faq.a2: The process is very intuitive and takes less than a couple of minutes. You just need to download our free mobile app and create your companion's digital profile. Then, simply bring your phone close to the physical tag for the NFC technology to detect it instantly. The tag and the app will be linked immediately.
faq.q3: Does it work on any type of phone?
faq.a3: Yes, the ecosystem is designed to be fully accessible. Scanning the tag works with any modern smartphone (iOS or Android) that has an integrated NFC reader. Whoever finds your companion does not need to have the app installed to read the tag and contact you.
faq.q4: Do I need to recharge the Paw Tag medal battery?
faq.a4: No, you won't have to worry about cables or remembering to charge it every week. Paw Tag works with a standard replaceable watch battery that lasts approximately one year. When it runs out, you can easily replace it yourself at a very low cost.
faq.q5: What exactly happens when someone scans my companion's tag?
faq.a5: If your companion ever gets lost, the person who finds them only needs to bring their phone close to the medal. Doing so will instantly open an emergency screen in their browser (potentially even showing a video of you giving instructions), displaying only your essential contact information and urgent medical data to facilitate a quick and safe return home.

footer.brand_desc: Comprehensive care from love and respect for those who bring joy to our lives.
footer.geo: 📍 Available in all Mexico. Soon all over LATAM.

activar.meta_desc: Link your Paw Tag quickly to start protecting your companion's vital information.

manual.meta_desc: Setup instructions, usage, and frequently asked questions for the new Paw Tag (Wireless Tag).
manual.title: User Manual
manual.subtitle: Setup guide for the new Paw Tag version
manual.model_note: These instructions are for the new version (Wireless Tag) with support for Google Find Hub and Apple Find My.
manual.android_req: Compatible only with Android 9+. Does not support Huawei devices.
manual.locked_note: Once paired, the tag is locked to that account.
manual.android_sec1_title: 1. Log in Google Find Hub APP
manual.android_sec1_step1: Go to Google Play and search for 'Find Hub'.
manual.android_sec1_step2: Install the app or click to 'Update'.
manual.android_sec1_step3: Hit 'Open' or click on the Find Hub app icon on your phone home page.
manual.android_sec1_step4: Log in to the Find Hub page with your Google Account. You might want to select the checkbox that says 'Don't ask again'. If you have more than one account, your item will link to the primary account setup on your phone.
manual.android_sec1_step5: You might be asked to allow some permissions. Review the terms and continue.
manual.android_sec2_desc: Hold the circular dot on the device's power button for 3 seconds. When you hear a 'ring-ring' chime, it indicates that the device has been powered on (Refer to the diagram).
manual.android_sec3_step1: Make sure your phone's Bluetooth is ON and you are connected to the Internet.
manual.android_sec3_step2: Bring your tag close to your phone and wait for the pop-up to show 'Wireless tag'. On the pop-up bottom sheet, hit 'Connect'. Read and accept the prompts to complete the pairing.
manual.android_sec4_1_1: If you know that the item is somewhere in the house, Find Hub will indicate its signal strength so you can narrow down its location.
manual.android_sec4_1_2: If your device is within wireless signal range, select your device in the 'Find Hub' app and tap 'Play Sound' to locate it. Once found, tap 'Stop Sound' to silence the device.
manual.android_sec4_1_3: Google Assistant on your Google Home or Pixel Watch is here to help. Try prompts such as, 'Hey Google, find my wallet' or 'Hey Google, find my keys'.
manual.android_sec4_2_desc: For the moments when you're really far away from an item, the Find Hub network is here to help. Android devices all over the world — like tablets, phones, and laptops — will work together to support you in finding your items.
manual.android_sec4_3_desc: When your item is lost, select the device in the 'Google Find Hub' app and tap 'Mark as lost'. You will receive a notification when the location becomes available. You can leave your contact information for the finder of your item to contact you.
manual.android_sec4_4_desc: Select the device in the 'Find Hub' app, then tap 'Share Device'. After the accessory owner confirms sharing, the accessory will be visible on the list in the Find Hub app. Both accounts will see the location updates and may ring the device locally.
manual.android_sec4_5_desc: Your safety comes first. Find Hub is built to deter unwanted tracking. If your Android phone notices an unfamiliar device moving with you, you will receive a notification. You can view the unknown device on a map, ring it to find it by sound, or get further help.
manual.android_sec4_6_desc: Your location data is end-to-end encrypted, meaning that Google can never view or access it. Only you can see where your items have been and currently are. Google does not use nor share your data.
manual.android_sec5_1_title: 5.1 Power on (Find my function is on)
manual.android_sec5_1_desc: Press and hold the function button for 3 seconds to power it on. A 'ring-ring' chime will indicate that the device is turned on. Remark: If the product is not linked with your Google account, it will close within 10 minutes automatically. You need to trigger the find my function again to continue pairing.
manual.android_sec5_2_title: 5.2 Power off (Find my function is off)
manual.android_sec5_2_desc: Hold the function button for 3 seconds then release it... you will hear two beeps indicating it is powered off. Note 1: If you press it for less than 3 seconds or more than 5 seconds, the device won't shut down. Note 2: Setting a shutdown timer is designed to prevent accidental shutdowns, ensuring the device can continue to locate effectively.
manual.android_sec5_3_desc: Press the function button two times (you will hear one beep), then press the function button four times rapidly, and then hold it a fifth time until you hear a 'ring-ring' chime. Now the device is reset.
manual.android_sec6_step1: Insert the shell opening tool into the shell opening.
manual.android_sec6_step2: Rotate the shell opening tool 90 degrees to open the shell (No need to fully open the entire shell).
manual.android_sec6_step3: Use your fingernails at the small gap on the side of your Item Locator to carefully open the case.
manual.android_sec6_step4: Replace the battery with a new CR2032 3V battery (Positive pole with the text upward).
manual.android_sec6_step5: Put the lanyard into the column, then close the cover.
manual.android_sec7_q1: Can't Connect My Device With Google's Find Hub App:
manual.android_sec7_m1: Method 1: Restart your phone's Bluetooth and cellular network, shut down and restart the device, then wait for the pop-up to show 'Wireless tag'.
manual.android_sec7_m2: Method 2: Reset your device, shut down and restart the device. Then wait for the pop-up to show 'Wireless tag'.
manual.android_sec7_m3: Method 3: Please restart your phone, shut down and restart the device, then wait for the pop-up to show 'Wireless tag'.
manual.android_sec7_m4: Method 4 (Update Software): Access phone settings > 'System' > 'Software updates' > 'System update', then check and update your phone's system to the newest version.
manual.android_sec7_m5:
Method 5 (Google Services):
• Step 1: Access phone settings and find 'Google service & preferences'.
• Step 2: Access 'All services'.
• Step 3: Find 'Connected devices & Sharing'.
• Step 4: Access 'Devices' and find 'Available devices nearby'. If you can see 'Wireless tag', click it and wait for the pop-up.
• Step 5: If you cannot see it, shut down and restart the device, or reset it, until it shows up.
manual.ios_sec1_desc: To prevent accidental shutdowns that could result in the device being unable to locate, we have re-upgraded the startup and shutdown methods.
manual.ios_sec1_1_1: Power On: Hold the circular dot on the device's power button for 3 seconds. When you hear a 'ring-ring' chime, it indicates that the device has been powered on (Refer to the diagram).
manual.ios_sec1_1_2: Power Off: Press the device button twice, and you will hear a confirmation sound. Then press and hold the power button for 3 seconds (If you press it for less than 3 seconds or more than 3 seconds, the device will not power off), then release it... you will hear two beeps indicating it is powered off.
manual.ios_sec1_1_remark: Remark: Setting a shutdown timer is designed to prevent accidental shutdowns, ensuring the device can continue to locate effectively.
manual.ios_sec1_2_desc: To use the Apple Find My app to locate the Item Locator, the latest version of iOS, iPadOS, or macOS is recommended. The Find My app on Apple Watch requires the latest version of watchOS.
manual.ios_sec2_1_desc: Open the Find My app on your supported iPhone or iPad. Allow notifications from the app.
manual.ios_sec2_2_desc: Power on your Locator, select the 'Items' tab, then tap the 'Add Item' tab and choose 'Other Supported Items'. Make sure your Item Locator is close to your phone. Wait until you see the pop-up 'HEY TAG', then tap 'Connect'. Choose a recognizable name and emoji for your Item Locator and tap 'Continue'. Find My will ask for confirmation to add your Locator to your Apple ID — tap 'Continue'. Tap 'Finish' and your Locator will be set up and ready to be connected to whatever item you wish to locate (e.g., your keys).
manual.ios_sec3_1_desc: When your item is nearby, tap 'Play Sound' to make your Locator beep so you can find it. When it is out of range, you can find the Locator's Last Known Location.
manual.ios_sec3_2_desc: You will receive a notification when you leave your Locator behind and it is no longer in range of your device. Note: 'Notify When Found' can only be activated when your Locator is out of range.
manual.ios_sec3_3_desc: The people you've shared it with will be able to locate it, and they won't receive unidentified-tracking notifications. To use Share Items, make sure you and your friends have installed iOS 17, iPadOS 17 or macOS 14 Sonoma public betas or newer.
manual.ios_sec3_4_desc: When your item is lost, enable lost mode. You will receive a notification when the location becomes available. You can leave a phone number or email address for the finder of your item to contact you. Note: When 'Lost Mode' is enabled, your Locator is locked and no one else can pair with it. DO NOT REMOVE ITEM in the app, or your Item Locator will be permanently locked and cannot be used by anyone.
manual.ios_sec3_5_desc: Make sure your item is nearby. You can remove the item in the app; it should make a 'ring-ring' chime indicating it is removed successfully, then the item can be used by other people. Note: When 'Lost Mode' is enabled, DO NOT REMOVE ITEM IN THE APP, otherwise your Item Locator will be locked and no one else can pair with it.
manual.ios_sec3_6_desc: When your item is out of the range of your phone and your Locator is removed from the Find My app, the device cannot be used by other people. You will need to reset your locator so it can be used by others. How to restore factory settings: Press the function button two times (you will hear one beep). Then press the function button four times rapidly and hold it a fifth time until you hear a 'ring-ring' chime. Now the device is reset and can be paired to a new Apple ID account as a new device.
manual.ios_sec3_7_desc: If any Find My network accessory separated from its owner is seen moving with you over time, you'll be notified in one of two ways: If you have an iPhone/iPad: Find My will send a notification to your Apple device (Available on iOS or iPadOS 14.5 or later). If you don't have an iOS device or smartphone: The Find My network accessory will emit a sound when it is moved if it has been away from its owner for a period of time.
manual.ios_sec4_desc:
• Battery: CR2032 3V (Replaceable)
• Working Voltage: 2.8V - 3.3V
• Capacity: 220mAh
• Wireless Range: 10 - 30 meters
• Buzzer: Max 100 dB
• Product Size: 32 x 7.9 mm
manual.ios_sec5_1: • Power On: Press the function button for 3 seconds. A 'ring-ring' chime indicates it is on. If not linked to an Apple ID, it automatically shuts off after 10 minutes.
manual.ios_sec5_2: • Power Off: Hold for 3 seconds, then release. You will hear two beeps.
manual.ios_sec5_3: • Enable Learn More Information: Press the button two times rapidly to hear a confirmation sound. This enables someone who finds the device to see the serial number or your contact details (if Lost Mode is on).
manual.ios_sec5_4: • Factory Reset: Press 2 times (1 beep), then press 4 times rapidly, and hold on the 5th time until the 'ring-ring' chime.
manual.ios_sec6_step1: Insert the shell opening tool into the shell opening.
manual.ios_sec6_step2: Rotate the tool 90 degrees to open the shell (No need to fully open the entire case).
manual.ios_sec6_step3: Use your fingernails at the small gap on the side to carefully open it.
manual.ios_sec6_step4: Replace with a new CR2032 3V battery (Positive pole with text facing upward).
manual.ios_sec6_step5: Place the lanyard into the column and close the cover.
manual.ios_sec7_q1: Why is my device's location not updating?
manual.ios_sec7_a1: If there are many Apple devices nearby, location reports every 2–5 minutes as users pass by. If the device hasn't moved, built-in sensors detect this and it enters a sleep mode, updating from 30 minutes to several hours. The location shown might be older, but it means the item hasn't moved.
manual.ios_sec7_q2: Pairing fails with the Find My App:
manual.ios_sec7_a2: Pairing links to Apple servers and can fail due to network issues. Try switching networks (WiFi to mobile data), reset the item, and try repairing patiently.
manual.warning_choking: ⚠️ INGESTION HAZARD: This product contains a button cell or coin battery. DEATH or serious injury can occur if ingested. A swallowed battery can cause Internal Chemical Burns in as little as 2 hours.
manual.warning_kids: KEEP NEW AND USED BATTERIES OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN.
manual.warning_medical: Seek immediate medical attention if a battery is suspected to be swallowed or inserted inside any part of the body.
manual.warning_recycle: Remove and immediately recycle or dispose of used batteries according to local regulations. Do NOT dispose of batteries in household trash or incinerate.
manual.warning_discharge: Do not force discharge, recycle, disassemble, or heat above 50°C / 122°F. Doing so may result in injury due to venting, leakage, or explosion.
manual.warning_battery: Compatible battery type: CR2032 (Nominal battery voltage: DC 3V). Ensure correct polarity (+ and -) during installation.
manual.warning_mix: Do not mix old and new batteries, or different brands/types (alkaline, carbon-zinc, or rechargeable).
manual.fcc_compliance: This device complies with part 15 of the FCC Rules. Operation is subject to the following two conditions: 1) This device may not cause harmful interference, and 2) This device must accept any interference received, including interference that may cause undesired operation.
manual.fcc_note: Note: This equipment has been tested and found to comply with the limits for a Class B digital device, pursuant to part 15 of the FCC Rules. Any changes or modifications not expressly approved by the party responsible for compliance could void the user's authority to operate the equipment.
